senses.def_en: to fail to notice; ignore deliberately
senses.example: The art of being wise is the art of knowing what to overlook.
senses.example_zh: 做聪明人的诀窍，就是要懂得该忽略什么。

senses.example: He has been kind enough to overlook my fault.
senses.example_zh: 他很宽容，原谅了我的过错。

senses.example: We overlook the church from our house.
senses.example_zh: 我们可从家中俯瞰教堂。

senses.def_en: a quick look at sb. or sth. that does not allow you to see them clearly
senses.example: I caught only a glimpse of the president's car.
senses.example_zh: 我只瞥见了总统的汽车。

senses.example: I glimpsed her face in the crowd, but then she was gone.
senses.example_zh: 我在人群中瞥见她的脸，但随即她就消失了。

senses.example: The old gentleman just stood there glaring at the pickpocket and did not say a word.
senses.example_zh: 这位老先生只是站在那里对那个扒手怒目而视，一句话也没有说。

senses.example: The sun glared out of the blue sky.
senses.example_zh: 太阳在蓝天上发出强烈的光。

senses.def_en: a bright unpleasant light which hurts your eyes
senses.example: To avoid the glare of the sun, you had better wear a pair of sunglasses.
senses.example_zh: 为避免耀眼的阳光(照射)，最好戴上墨镜。

senses.example: She gave him an icy glare.
senses.example_zh: 她以冷冰冰的目光怒视着他。

senses.example: They were in the full glare of publicity.
senses.example_zh: 他们大肆炫耀。

derivatives: glaring a. 耀眼的；显眼的；怒目而视的

senses.example: We saw the lights of the little town gleaming in the distance.
senses.example_zh: 我们看见远处小镇的灯光在闪烁。

senses.def_en: (of an emotion) to appear or show through sb.'s eyes or expression (~with)
senses.example: The groom's eyes are gleaming with excitement.
senses.example_zh: 新郎的眼睛流露着激动的神情。

senses.def_en: a small pale light, esp. one that shines for a short time
senses.example: They saw the gleam of a lamp ahead.
senses.example_zh: 他们看到前面一盏灯的微光。

senses.example: A gleam of satisfaction crossed her face.
senses.example_zh: 她脸上闪现出满足的神情。

entries.mnemonic: glitt(发光)+er(表示反复动作)→闪耀

senses.def_en: to shine brightly with flashing points of light
senses.example: All that glitters is not gold.
senses.example_zh: [谚]闪光的不都是金子。

senses.example: The child was attracted by the glitter of the Christmas tree decorations.
senses.example_zh: 这孩子被圣诞树上闪闪发光的装饰品吸引住了。

senses.example: The glow of the setting sun is splendid; it is a pity that the dusk is fast approaching.
senses.example_zh: 夕阳无限好，只是近黄昏。

senses.def_en: a pink color in your face from exercising, being excited, healthy, etc.
senses.example: Her face has the glow of health.
senses.example_zh: 她脸色红润。

senses.example: She felt a glow of satisfaction at her son's achievements.
senses.example_zh: 儿子的成就让她感到心满意足。

senses.def_en: to shine brightly and steadily, esp. without a flame
senses.example: The metal glowed in the furnace.
senses.example_zh: 金属在熔炉里发出灼热的光。

senses.def_en: to have a warm, reddish color from exercise, emotion, etc.
senses.example: Her face was glowing with embarrassment.
senses.example_zh: 她尴尬得脸都红了。

senses.example: He is glowing with enthusiasm.
senses.example_zh: 他热情洋溢。

senses.def_en: to shine with a light that is not very bright
senses.example: There are some lights faintly glimmering in the distance.
senses.example_zh: 远处有几处明灭不定的微光。

senses.def_en: a light that is not very bright
senses.example: The sky was pink with the first glimmer of the dawn.
senses.example_zh: 清晨的第一缕曙光使天空呈现出淡红色。

extras.title: 表示“一瞥”的动词
extras.content: glance：匆匆看一眼或粗略扫一眼，强调动作 glimpse：指“瞥见”，强调结果，是 glance 的结果
